category_sources: source_facts.description describes wmctrl as a UNIX/Linux command-line tool for EWMH/NetWM interaction; official upstream README describes the same X window-manager control role.
display_name_sources: source_facts.name and official upstream README use wmctrl as the project name.
docs_sources: Official upstream homepage is the best available documentation surface; upstream README also says installed documentation is available via wmctrl -h.
repo_sources: source_facts.repo is empty; the Conservatory GitHub archive states wmctrl does not appear to have a publicly accessible upstream version-control repository, so no official source-control repo is returned.
tags_sources: source_facts.description and official upstream README identify EWMH/NetWM-compatible X Window Manager control from the command line.
